title: JVM Performance and Profiling
learningObjectives: Apply **JVM performance and profiling** with production-grade tests, tools, and measurable outcomes
Explain trade-offs between test types: unit, slice, integration, contract, e2e
Use profiling and benchmarking vocabulary correctly in senior interviews
Deliver behavioral answers with STAR structure tied to real architecture decisions
Run mock interviews with follow-up depth on testing and performance
Connect reliability practices from earlier phases to capstone scenarios

sections.title: Why JVM Performance and Profiling matters
sections.content: **Performance** answers need **measurement** literacy. **JMH** and **profilers** separate **data** from **folklore**—critical for **senior** **credibility**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - JVM Performance and Profiling
sections.content:
### Day 88 — **JVM** **performance**

### 1. Methodology
**Baseline**, **hypothesis**, **measure**, **fix**, **re-measure**.

### 2. Microbenchmarks
**JMH** **harness** **rules**.

### 3. Profilers
**CPU**, **alloc**, **wall** **time**.

### 4. GC
**Generational** **model**, **pause** **targets**.

### 5. JIT
**Warmup**, **tiered** **compilation**.

### 6. Story
**String** **concat** in **loop**—**StringBuilder** wins after **JIT** **stabilizes**; **prove** with **JMH**.

sections.title: Basic — JIT and GC headline
sections.output: JIT optimizes hot code; GC manages heap lifetimes

sections.title: Intermediate — JMH purpose
sections.output: JMH avoids DCE and accounts for warmup forks

sections.title: Advanced — Concat vs builder
sections.output: loop + creates many intermediate Strings; builder reuses buffer

sections.title: Profile workflow
Exercise — JVM Performance and Profiling (Day 88 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: JVM Performance & Profiling
2. Focus: JMH benchmark String + vs StringBuilder.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **JMH** **why** not **loop** **nanoTime**?
conceptual.answer: JIT dead-code elimination and unfair warmup—JMH controls forks, warmup, and blackholes. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **JVM performance**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. JIT dead-code elimination and unfair warmup—JMH controls forks, warmup, and blackholes. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. JIT dead-code elimination and unfair warmup—JMH controls forks, warmup, and blackholes. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

conceptual.question: **Allocation** **pressure** **symptoms**?
conceptual.answer: Young GC churn, high allocation rate in profiler—often hotter than algorithm big-O on moderate n. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **JVM performance**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Young GC churn, high allocation rate in profiler—often hotter than algorithm big-O on moderate n. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Young GC churn, high allocation rate in profiler—often hotter than algorithm big-O on mode For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

conceptual.question: **async-profiler** modes?
conceptual.answer: CPU wall vs alloc—pick question first then profile mode. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **JVM Performance and Profiling** deploy correlates with **5xx** spike.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(2) Root cause** **Config** regression or **dependency** **saturation**.

**(3) Fix** Rollback or **toggle** **flag**; restore **SLO** then root-cause.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Tool | Recall |
|---|---|
| JMH | Microbench |
| async-profiler | CPU alloc |
| JFR | Flight recording |
| jstack | Threads |
| MAT | Heap dump |
| GC log | Pause analysis |
| -Xlog:gc | Unified logging |
| Blackhole | JMH DCE |
| Amdahl | Parallel limit |
| p99 | Tail matters |
